Arabidopsis is a small flowering plant that belongs to the family Brassicaceae. It has been studied extensively for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.

The bioactive constituents of Arabidopsis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities.
